mnemonics.title: EVM Key Metrics
mnemonics.content: P-E-A-R-L: Planned (PV), Earned (EV), Actual (AC), Variance (SV, CV), Index (SPI, CPI), EAC/BAC/ETC - Remember these eight metrics form the core of Earned Value Analysis.

mnemonics.title: ISO/IEC 25010 Quality Characteristics
mnemonics.content: FRUEMPC (FRue-EMP-C): Functional suitability, Reliability, Usability, Efficiency, Maintainability, Portability, Compatibility (Security added as separate)

mnemonics.title: Evaluation Framework Steps
mnemonics.content: D-A-M-I: Define criteria, Assign weights, Measure performance, Interpret results - A systematic approach to project evaluation

acronyms.usage: Quantitative technique integrating cost, schedule, and scope measurements to assess project performance
Measures cost efficiency: CPI = EV/AC; <1 over budget, >1 under budget
Measures schedule efficiency/PV; <: SPI = EV1 behind schedule, >1 ahead of schedule
Projected total cost: EAC = BAC/CPI or EAC = AC + ETC
Original approved budget for the entire project
Formal evaluation after project completion to assess objective achievement and document learnings
Quantitative method: Total Score = Σ(weight_i × score_i) for multi-criteria evaluation

visualTips.visualization: Imagine a three-column table: Planned Work (PV), Actual Work Done (EV), Money Spent (AC). Compare columns to see variances. Green when EV matches or exceeds AC and PV; Red when variances are negative.
Use a 2×2 matrix: Horizontal axis = Cost (Under/Over), Vertical axis = Schedule (Ahead/Behind). Four quadrants show project health status clearly.

Arrange eight characteristics in a circle with 'Functional Suitability' at top, connecting to adjacent characteristics showing interdependencies. Each characteristic branches to 2-3 sub-characteristics.

analogies.analogy: Think of a bank account: PV is what you planned to deposit, AC is what you actually withdrew, EV is what you actually earned. Variance shows whether you're richer or poorer than planned.

Like a doctor's checkup: regular vitals (cost, schedule, quality) measured against healthy baselines. Red means 'admit to hospital' (major intervention), Yellow means 'monitor medication' (close watching), Green means 'healthy' (on track).
Like calculating GPA: Each course (criterion) has credits (weight) and grades (scores). Your GPA (overall score) combines all, giving a single performance metric that reflects what's most important.
